# Designing the Course Feeder Workbook

This chapter defines the **standard feeder Excel workbook** used to generate a complete Quarto-based course website. The workbook separates **course content**, **delivery logistics**, and **grading structure** into clearly defined sheets.

This separation is intentional:
* It mirrors how universities think about courses.
* It allows partial automation with AI.
* It prevents accidental coupling between pedagogy and logistics.

## Workbook Overview

Based on the official benchmark (`Course-Schedule.xlsx`), the feeder workbook contains several essential sheets. The primary ones you will interact with are:

| Sheet Name       | Purpose                                    |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `Course Details` | What is taught (modules, lectures, topics) |
| `Sections`       | How and when it is taught                  |
| `Grade`          | How students are evaluated                 |

*(Other supplementary sheets like `Calendar`, `Day List`, and `Deliverables` are used for mapping out specific term dates and schedules).*

## Sheet 1 — `Course Details` (Core Content Sheet)

### Purpose
This sheet defines the **academic structure of the course**. It is the only sheet required to generate Lecture Notes, Presentations, Labs, and Assignments using our Quarto workflow.

### Required Columns
The `Course Details` sheet **must contain exactly** the following columns:

| Column        | Description                      |
| ------------- | -------------------------------- |
| `Modules`     | Module identifier (e.g. `Module 0`, `Module 1`)|
| `Lecture`     | Lecture identifier (e.g. `L0.1`, `L1.1`)       |
| `Title`       | Lecture title                    |
| `Description` | One–two paragraph description    |
| `Online`      | `TRUE` / `FALSE` or blank        |
| `On-Campus`   | `TRUE` / `FALSE` or blank        |

### Example Rows

| Modules | Lecture | Title | Description | Online | On-Campus |
| ------- | ------- | ----- | ----------- | ------ | --------- |
| Module 0| L0.1    | GitHub Education and Student Account Creations | GitHub, Github Classrooms; Jupyter Notebooks, Quarto overview. | | |
| Module 1| L1.1    | Introduction to Big Data and Cloud | Course logistics, terminology, development setups. | | |

## Sheet 2 — `Sections` (Delivery Logistics)

### Purpose
This sheet captures **when and by whom** the course is delivered. It is used for syllabus generation and schedule pages.

### Required Columns
| Column         | Description                       |
| -------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `Section`      | Section identifier (e.g., A1, B1) |
| `Location`     | Classroom or Zoom link            |
| `Instructor`   | Instructor name                   |
| `Email @ bu`   | Instructor's BU email             |
| `Day`          | Day of week                       |
| `Time`         | Start and end time                |

## Sheet 3 — `Grade` (Evaluation Structure)

### Purpose
This sheet defines the **grading logic** independently of content. It supports automatic syllabus grading tables.

### Required Columns (Key Elements)
| Column         | Description            |
| -------------- | ---------------------- |
| `Deliverables` | Category name (e.g. Class Activity, Assignments) |
| `Count_norman` | Number of items        |
| `Max_Points`   | Max points per item or category |

## Automating the Workbook Generation

Instead of creating this Excel file by hand, you can use a Python script to automatically generate a blank template populated with these exact sheets and columns. This ensures you never have a typo in a column name that breaks the downstream Quarto generators.

### Workbook Generator Script

Create a file named `generate_workbook.py` and run it to produce a pristine `Course-Schedule.xlsx` file ready for you to fill in:

```python
import pandas as pd

def generate_blank_workbook(filename="Course-Schedule.xlsx"):
    # Define the sheets and their exact columns
    sheets = {
        "Course Details": ["Modules", "Lecture", "Title", "Description", "Online", "On-Campus"],
        "Sections": ["Section", "Location", "Instructor", "Email @ bu", "Day", "Time"],
        "Grade": ["Deliverables", "Count_norman", "Max_Points", "Class Activity", "Count", "Points", "Max Points"],
        "Day List": ["A1", "A2", "A3"],
        "Calendar": ["Term", "First Name", "Last Name", "BU email", "Weekday", "Time", "W01D1", "W02D1"],
        "Deliverables": ["Term", "Deliverable type", "Weekday", "W01D6", "W02D6"]
    }

    with pd.ExcelWriter(filename, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
        for sheet_name, columns in sheets.items():
            # Create an empty dataframe with the exact columns
            df = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)
            
            # For Course Details, we can optionally add a couple of blank module rows as a starter
            if sheet_name == "Course Details":
                df.loc[0] = ["Module 0", "L0.1", "", "", "", ""]
                df.loc[1] = ["Module 0", "L0.2", "", "", "", ""]
                
            df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=sheet_name, index=False)
            
    print(f"✅ Successfully generated {filename} with all required sheets.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    generate_blank_workbook()
```

## AI Automation (Optional)

Once your `Course Details` sheet is generated, and you have filled in the `Modules` and `Lecture` columns, you can use the AI Metadata Scripts discussed in **Chapter 8 (Automating with AI)** to automatically populate the `Title` and `Description` columns using OpenAI or Gemini!
